distance_km.note: The McMurdo Marathon is a full marathon distance (26.2 miles / 42.195 km), not 20 km. Search results confirm this is a marathon event held annually.

elevation_m.note: Search results do not provide specific elevation gain data for the McMurdo Marathon course.

country.note: McMurdo Station is located in Antarctica, confirmed by multiple sources.

region_state_province.note: The race is held at McMurdo Station on the shore of McMurdo Sound, not simply 'Ross Dependency'. The database value is incomplete/imprecise.

discipline.note: The McMurdo event is described as a 'Run and Ski Marathon' with options for both running and skiing the course, not classic skiing only.

typical_participants.note: One source mentions 52 participants in 2011, but insufficient data across multiple years to establish a reliable 'typical' number. The claim of 50 is plausible but not definitively verified.

year_founded.note: The McMurdo Marathon may have been the first marathon held in Antarctica in January 1995, not 2003. Wikipedia states it is now held annually.

official_website.note: Search results do not confirm https://www.usap.gov as the official McMurdo Marathon website. The USAP (United States Antarctic Program) operates McMurdo Station, but no dedicated race website is verified in search results.

typical_race_date.note: The McMurdo Marathon is held in January during the Antarctic summer season, not mid-October. October is austral spring, but the race occurs during the warmer summer months when McMurdo's population swells.
